data_source: Rfam CURRENT Rfam.seed.gz — consensus secondary structure (SS_cons) lines from Stockholm-format seed alignments for 4230 RNA families. Downloaded from https://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/Rfam/CURRENT/Rfam.seed.gz. Additional structures fetched via Rfam REST API for 30 representative families. Tiled to reach 1M chars.
encoding_notes: Two representations analysed: (1) WUSS notation (13 symbols) — the full Rfam annotation including '<>()', '.', '_', ',', ':', '-', '[]', '{}' where different bracket pairs denote different pseudoknot levels and different single-stranded characters encode loop type context; (2) Normalised dot-bracket (3 symbols) — all bracket pairs collapsed to '()', all unpaired/gap characters collapsed to '.' — this is the canonical dot-bracket representation. Results below are for WUSS (primary) with normalised as secondary. HIGH structure score expected: bracket-matching grammar imposes long-range constraints — every '(' must be followed eventually by ')'.

shuffled_control.note: Shuffling destroys all sequential structure; structure_score collapses to ~0, confirming the signal is genuine sequential constraint

normalised_dot_bracket.encoding: All bracket pairs -> '()'; all unpaired/gap chars ('.', '_', ',', ':', '-') -> '.'

shuffled_control.note: Perfectly shuffled; parentheses become random so structure score collapses

interpretation: Both encodings show strong sequential structure. WUSS: structure_score=0.681 (very high), H3 drops from H0=2.92 to 0.93 bits — a 68% reduction in uncertainty given 2 preceding characters. Normalised dot-bracket: structure_score=0.532 — still high. MI decays slowly (MI=0.06 bits even at lag=500 in WUSS), indicating long-range bracket-pairing correlations. Shuffled control collapses to ~0 in both cases, confirming all structure is sequential. This is expected from bracket-matching grammar: open brackets are statistically constrained to be followed by matching close brackets, creating deterministic long-range dependencies. RNA dot-bracket is among the most structured natural encodings measurable.
